characters.name: Abyzou
characters.description: The AbyzouBiographical InformationAlias:The Tooth FairyStatus:DeceasedPhysical DescriptionSpecies:DemonHair color:BlackRelationshipsAllies:PandoraMolochEnemies:Ichabod CraneAbbie MillsJenny MillsPaul RevereAppearancesFirst appeared:"The Sisters Mills"Gallery TheAbyzouwas a creature summoned byPandorathat steals the souls of children. It was also known commonly as the Tooth Fairy. After the Abyzou was summoned fromPandora's Box, the creature targeted children with loose teeth inSleepy Hollow. One of its first targets was a little girl calledTessa Paynter. As the creature fed, the girlssisterwatched. Later, another target was a little boy. The creature hid in a closet as the boy and his mother was getting ready for bed. She opens the closet door but doesn't see the Abyzou inside. Gregory does and calls out, but when she reopens the closet door, the Abyzou is gone. Once the mother leaves,Gregoryturns on his light and approaches the closet. Gregory opens the door... and finds nothing. The Abyzou comes up behind him and Gregory runs out, screaming. The following night, the creature returns to claim Gregory. Gregory's tooth starts to bleed, and the Abyzou enters the room. he screams and outside,JennyandAbbiehear him. the boy holds out the silver coin and the Abyzou retreats outside. Gregory goes after it and tells Abbie and Jenny where to swing. Jenny manages to hit a sprinkler and outlines the creature momentarily. While Gregory runs inside to his sister, Jenny sprays the Abyzou with a garden hose. A hooded figure emerges from the shadow and knocks Jenny down, and Abbie goes to help her sister. She stabs the Abyzou with a silver blade but it has no effect. The Abyzou knocks her into a tree and runs off, and Jenny runs to her unconscious sister. The following night, Saffron goes to her treehouse to set a trap and puts Tessa's teeth on the floor, and waits for the Abyzou. It drops in and starts to attack her, andIchabodarrives and fires a shot of silver nitrate. The substance reveals the Abyzou, and Ichabod kicks it out the window to wear Jenny is waiting with an axe. The monster runs off and Jenny goes after it. Ichabod sends Saffron inside as Jenny calls that the silver nitrate wore off. They head into the forest, and Ichabod manages to fire a shot, briefly revealing the creature. He reloads and prepares to shoot, and the invisible Abyzou knocks him to the floor. He spots the creature's footsteps and throws some silver nitrate on it, revealing its location. Jenny manages to stab it in its weak spot, and the Abyzou collapses and then dissolves into dust.[1]

characters.name: Abigail Adams
characters.description: Abigail AdamsBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedProfession:SleuthPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:FemaleHair color:Dark BrownEye color:HazelRelationshipsFamily:John Adams †(husband)Allies:Katrina CraneEnemies:James ColbyAppearancesPortrayed by:Michelle TrachtenbergFirst appeared:"Pittura Infamante"Gallery Abigail Adamswas one of the closest friends of the lateKatrina Crane. Abigail was an amateur sleuth and was responsible for the capture of the lateJames Colby.

characters.name: Jesse Adams
characters.description: Jesse AdamsBiographical InformationProfession:Antique shop ownerPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:MaleRelationshipsAllies:Jenny Mills,Frank IrvingEnemies:The Shadow WarriorsAppearancesPortrayed by:Mark Jeffrey MillerFirst appeared:""Necromancer"(only appearance)"GalleryJesse Adamsis a friend ofJenny Millsthat ownsAdams Antiquities; an antiques and pawn shop. Frank Irving and Jenny Mills were looking for anobject that could bring evil spirits and demonsto come alive because Moloch wants to bring evil being to the world becauseIchabod CraneandAbbie Millscaptured the Horsemen of Death. when they went to his shop, they found that it was robbed and he was shot and wounded by the Shadow Warriors, who stole the object.

characters.name: Ancitif
characters.actor_name: Judd Lormand(Vendor)Karen Beyer(Woman)Lyndie Greenwood(Jenny)Michael Roark(Devon)Nicholas Gonzalez(Luke)Amandla Stenberg/Nydia McFadden(Macey)
characters.description: AncitifBiographical InformationBirth:HellStatus:UnknownAffiliation:MolochPhysical DescriptionSpecies:DemonRelationshipsAllies:MolochFour Horsemen of the ApocalypseEnemies:Jenny MillsAbbie MillsIchabod CraneFrank IrvingAugust CorbinAppearancesPortrayed by:Judd Lormand(Vendor)Karen Beyer(Woman)Lyndie Greenwood(Jenny)Michael Roark(Devon)Nicholas Gonzalez(Luke)Amandla Stenberg/Nydia McFadden(Macey)First appeared:"The Golem"Last appeared:"The Vessel"GalleryAncitifis a demon of possession who attackedMacey Irvingin the episode "The Vessel". In the 17th cenury, Ancitif and other lustful demons possessed a group of nuns in Louviers.[1]He was later expelled using theFrench Lantern.[2] In the 21st century, Ancitif resurfaced and possessedJenny Mills. Upon noticing that there was something wrong with her, SheriffAugust Corbinlocked her up using a barrier of salt. He then documented her possession until he managed to find a way to expel Ancitif from Jenny. Even though Ancitif was gone, Jenny still had thoughts about hurting her sister, what was originally Ancitifs thoughts.[2]

characters.actor_name: Judd Lormand(Vendor)Karen Beyer(Woman)Lyndie Greenwood(Jenny)Michael Roark(Devon)Nicholas Gonzalez(Luke)Amandla Stenberg/Nydia McFadden(Macey)

characters.name: Angel
characters.description: AngelsBiographical InformationStatus:ActiveProfession:Protectors of Earth.Affiliation:GoodPhysical DescriptionSpecies:AngelsAppearancesFirst appeared:"Paradise Lost"GalleryAngelsare celestial, winged beings who serve Heaven. Not much is known about them, except that they rarely fight againstdemonic forces. The only Angel to appear on the show so far isOrion. Their total number remains a mystery. It is suggested that angels were supposedly created by God in heaven some time before the time of the dinosaurs. At God's request, the angels bowed before humanity and sought to ensure that the forces of good would prevail over evil. However, one angel in particular refused to follow their commands, to the point of uniting several of his celestial companions in a rebellion that failed. As punishment, Lucifer and his troops would be banished by God himself and the Archangel Michael to Hell, where they would prevail as their prisoners. An angel of death is mentioned as having taken the souls of all the firstborn in the last of the Ten Plagues of Egypt.

characters.name: Ansel
characters.description: AnselBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedProfession:Co-founder ofDreyfuss Enterprises(formerly)Physical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:MaleHair color:BaldEye color:BrownRelationshipsAllies:Malcolm Dreyfuss(formerly)Enemies:Malcolm DreyfussJobeIchabod CraneDiana ThomasAppearancesPortrayed by:Bjorn DupatyFirst appeared:"Blood from a Stone"Gallery Anselappears in season four ofSleepy Hollow. He was originally partners withMalcolm Dreyfussand was co-founder of the company, and in fact, overshadowed Malcolm from the onset. Then Malcolm made a deal with a demon to get rid of Ansel. Ansel was then banished toHell, but once he escaped, he learned how to use sigils branded upon his body by demons and weaponized them in order to obtain thePhilosopher's Stone. As young men, Ansel was best friends withMalcolm Dreyfuss. The two of them created a business together; Malcolm was the brains of the operation and Ansel dealt with the social part of the business. Over time, people saw Ansel as the face of the business, and Malcolm became a kind of back ground shadow. Resenting their roles, Malcolm drank himself stupid, and drove off in a rage, but crashed his car. As he was dying, he begged for rescue, and a demon appeared. It made a deal with Malcolm; for Malcolm's soul, they will save him, and get rid of Ansel, leaving the business to Malcom.  Malcolm signs on the dotted line, and Jobe takes Ansel to Hell.

characters.name: Vivian Apfel
characters.description: Vivian ApfelBiographical InformationProfession:PotterPosition:Owner ofThe ClayhouseAffiliation:The ClayhousePhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:FemaleHair color:GreyAppearancesFirst appeared:"Sleepy Hollow, Volume 1"GalleryVivian Apfelwas a local potter and owner of her own studio,The Clayhouse, inSleepy Hollow. While working at her studio at night, something in her fireplace attacked. However, she was then cured of her blindness. She also gained healing abilities. She demonstrated this power by healing a man in a wheel chair. However, when she tried to heal a boy's broken arm, she lost control of her powers and killed the boy. Her arms were also stretched. She was then arrested by Sheriff/Police CaptainFrank Irving. Police LieutenantAbbie Millsand ConsultantIchabod Cranelearned that Vivian had getting her clay from the river banks of theHudson River.They figure that the bank had been infected by the ashes from a hundred witches of theOrder of the Blood Moonthat were scattered into the river[1]after its High Priestess,Serildawas blown to pieces inthe Munition Tunnels.[2]

characters.name: Armin
characters.description: ArminBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedProfession:HessianPosition:Shadow WarriorAffiliation:Shadow WarriorsPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:MaleHair color:BrownEye color:BlueRelationshipsAllies:GuntherHagenShadow WarriorsMolochEnemies:Wendel ClarkAbbie MillsIchabod CraneJenny MillsAppearancesPortrayed by:Kevin CassidyFirst appeared:"The Lesser Key of Solomon"GalleryArminis a mercenary for theShadow Warriors. He was killed byIchabod Cranewhen he was thrownThe Lesser Key of Solomon'sfiery portal. Armin, along with his partners,HagenandGunther, were ordered to search forJennifer Mills. They had leads that pointed toWendel Clark's bar. After Wendel denied she had been there,   Armin and his associates tortured him to death, but not before extracting the confirmation she had been there. To find the location of a book known wasThe Lesser Key of Solomon, he helped ambushthe Witnessesand Jenny by stealing their sextant from theTrout Lakecabin.

characters.name: Benedict Arnold
characters.description: Benedict ArnoldBiographical InformationBirth:Norwich, Connecticut ColonyProfession:Major GeneralBrigadier General(formerly)Affiliation:Continental ArmyBritish Army(formerly)Physical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:MaleHair color:WhiteRelationshipsFamily:Hannah Waterman King (mother)Benedict Arnold (father)Benedict Arnold, Absalom Arnold (brothers)Hannah Arnold, Mary Arnold, Elizabeth Arnold (sisters)Elizabeth Waterman (maternal grandmother)John Waterman (maternal grandfather)Benedict Arnold (paternal grandfather)Margaret Mansfield, Peggy Shippen (spouses)Benedict Arnold, Richard Arnold, Henry Arnold (children by Margaret) Edward Shippen Arnold, James Robertson Arnold, George Arnold, Sophia Matilda Arnold, William Fitch Arnold (children by Peggy)Allies:Ichabod Crane(formerly)Enemies:George WashingtonIchabod CraneAppearancesPortrayed by:Scott PoythressFirst appeared:"Root of All Evil"GalleryBenedict Arnold(January 14, 1741 - June 14, 1801) was a Major General in the Continental Army during the American Revolution. In 1780, under the influence of one ofJudas' shekels, Arnold defected and became a Brigadier General in the British Army. Benedict Arnold (father)Benedict Arnold, Absalom Arnold (brothers)Hannah Arnold, Mary Arnold, Elizabeth Arnold (sisters)Elizabeth Waterman (maternal grandmother)John Waterman (maternal grandfather)Benedict Arnold (paternal grandfather)Margaret Mansfield, Peggy Shippen (spouses) Elected as a Captain in the Connecticut militia in March 1775, Arnold found early success in the capture of Fort Ticonderoga from its British garrison on May 10 with the help of Ethan Allen. Arnold followed this up by playing key roles in the invasion of Quebec and the battles of Valcour Island, Ridgefield, and Saratoga. He also served as military governor of Montreal and Philadelphia. During this time, he developed a friendship withIchabod Crane. Crane found him to be a brave soldier who deeply loved his country. Despite his success, he felt that he was unfairly passed over for promotion by the Continental Congress.

Arnold followed this up by playing key roles in the invasion of Quebec and the battles of Valcour Island, Ridgefield, and Saratoga. He also served as military governor of Montreal and Philadelphia. During this time, he developed a friendship withIchabod Crane. Crane found him to be a brave soldier who deeply loved his country. characters.name: Banshee
characters.description: BansheeBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedPhysical DescriptionSpecies:BansheeGender:FemaleHair color:BrownRelationshipsEnemies:Ichabod CraneAbbie MillsJenny MillsJoseph CorbinAppearancesPortrayed by:CC IceFirst appeared:"Incommunicado"GalleryTheBansheeis a one-appearance characterr and who was the monster, but with a minor role, in the fifteenth episode of the third season of the series Sleepy Hollow. The Banshee is a folkloric creature known as a messenger of death, as it can absorb all the noise from the place where it is located and later unleash it in a shrill scream capable of killing anyone present. Possibly drawn by the call of the Hidden One, the Banshee is one of the many creatures that come to Sleepy Hollow, appearing one night in a garage, attracted by the noise of a band rehearsing. She absorbed the sound and released it in a powerful shriek that killed the three individuals.

characters.name: Barghest
characters.actor_name: Alexander WardBill Heck(asMitch Talbot)Brent McGee(asWilliam Bradford)
characters.description: BarghestBiographical InformationAlias:William Bradford(fake identity)Mitch Talbot(fake identity)Status:DeceasedProfession:Serial KillerPhysical DescriptionSpecies:DemonGender:MaleHair color:Black FurEye color:YellowRelationshipsEnemies:WitnessesDiana ThomasAppearancesPortrayed by:Alexander WardBill Heck(asMitch Talbot)Brent McGee(asWilliam Bradford)First appeared:"Loco Parentis"Gallery TheBarghestwas a shape shifting demonic wolf-like creature that primarily hunted young girls, and particularly, youngWitnesses. When the Mayflower traveled to the New World, "the settlers had unwittingly brought something with them from the Old World. A monster, stowed away in the bowels of the Mayflower, and came to life upon their arrival in Plymouth. The Barghest, a wolf-demon that originated from the forests of medieval Europe."

characters.name: Arthur Bernard
characters.description: Arthur BenardBiographical InformationAlias:CiceroProfession:Freedom FighterPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:MaleHair color:Black(Wig)WhiteEye color:BrownRelationshipsAllies:Ichabod Crane,Katrina Crane,FreemasonsEnemies:Moloch,Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse,Banastre TarletonAppearancesPortrayed by:Tongayi ChirisaFirst appeared:"The Sin Eater"GalleryArthur Bernardwas a freed slave who was charged with treason whenTarletonbelieved him to be the revolutionary author spreading propaganda against the British under the pseudonym Cicero. (Wig)White Bernard was believed by Colonel Tarleton to be the propagandist, Cicero, who had posted multiple anti-British pamphlets. They found him inside of his home, but he denied he knew Cicero.

characters.name: Big Ash
characters.description: Big AshBiographical InformationStatus:AliveProfession:BikerPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:MaleHair color:BlackRelationshipsAllies:Ichabod CraneNick HawleyAppearancesPortrayed by:Eddie SpearsFirst appeared:"And The Abyss Gazes Back"Gallery Big Ashis a Shawnee contact ofNick Hawley. He and his fellow Shawnee run a motorcycle repair shop. Ash was displeased with Hawley for selling off the tribal mask, as Hawley dishonored his agreement to keep the mask. Nick Hawleysought out Big Ash and his Shawnee gang for his assistance in searching for aWendigocure. Big Ash was first cold with Hawley as Hawley had sold theSisuro Transformation Mashwhich Big Ash had sold to him on the condition that he doesn't sell it. Hawley did and bought a boat with the money he made. Hawley initially assumed thatIchabod Cranewas too socially awkward to communicate with Big Ash. But Crane's mention ofSquire Boonebecame the key for Big Ash to agree to help them find a Wendigo cure forJoe Corbin. Big Ash then pointed Ichabod in the direction of Frank, their Shaman.[1]

characters.name: Daniel Boone
characters.description:
Daniel BooneBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedProfession:ExplorerPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:MaleRelationshipsFamily:Squire Boone †(brother)Allies:Ichabod CraneAppearancesPortrayed by:Robin StrasdsFirst appeared:"And the Abyss Gazes Back"Last appeared:"The Art of War"Gallery Daniel Boonewas frontiersman and friend of Continental Army CaptainIchabod Crane. He was one of the first folk heroes of the United States. Boone became famous for his exploration and settlement of what is now Kentucky, In 1778, following their encampment at Valley Forge, Boone's brother Squire, suffered a case of "nostalgia," "when a soldier loses the ability to see anything beyond the horrors of war."  Rumor had it he had indulged in cannibalism during the encampment, and was afflicted with the curse of theWendigo; "A skin-walker from Shawnee and Algonquian legend, which possesses both human and bestial attributes and partakes in the act of 
cannibalism. Its feeding is triggered by its lust for human blood, once the appetite is triggered, the transformation begins. The beast can only return to human form after the consumption of human organs."   To help his brother defeat the curse, Boone spent a year among the Shawnee to find a cure. He was successful, but Boone received scars from his brother's attack that forced him to wear a racoon/beaver pelt hat to hide them.[1]

He was successful, but Boone received scars from his brother's attack that forced him to wear a racoon/beaver pelt hat to hide them.[1]

characters.name: John Wilkes Booth
characters.description: DemonHumanJohn Wilkes BoothBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedProfession:MurdererPhysical DescriptionSpecies:Human(formerly)DemonGender:MaleHair color:Stringy hairRelationshipsFamily:Seeley Booth(descendant)Allies:Malcolm DreyfussEnemies:Ichabod CraneJake WellsAlex NorwoodDiana ThomasJenny MillsAppearancesPortrayed by:Alexander Ward(demon)Adrian Bond(human)First appeared:"Columbia"Gallery John Wilkes Boothwas the demon in question who debuted in the Season Four premiere, "Columbia", and the first monster featured in Season Four. John summoned the demon into his body by using black magic. The demons was summoned to help destroy the Union. Over time, the American flag became a trigger that activated the killer instinct within the demon. During a play, John saw an American flag and ended up killing Abraham Lincoln. But Mary Todd Lincoln, sealed the demon away in an underground chamber at the Lincoln Memorial.[1]

characters.description: DemonHumanJohn Wilkes BoothBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedProfession:MurdererPhysical DescriptionSpecies:Human(formerly)DemonGender:MaleHair color:Stringy hairRelationshipsFamily:Seeley Booth(descendant)Allies:Malcolm DreyfussEnemies:Ichabod CraneJake WellsAlex NorwoodDiana ThomasJenny MillsAppearancesPortrayed by:Alexander Ward(demon)Adrian Bond(human)First appeared:"Columbia"Gallery John Wilkes Boothwas the demon in question who debuted in the Season Four premiere, "Columbia", and the first monster featured in Season Four. characters.name: Seeley Booth
characters.description: Seeley BoothBiographical InformationStatus:AliveProfession:FBI Special AgentUS Army Ranger (former)Position:FBI Supervisory Special AgentFBI Liaison to the Jeffersonian InstituteFBI Academy instructor(briefly)Prison Inmate(briefly)Sergeant Major/Master Sergeant (formerly)Affiliation:FBI Washington Field OfficeJeffersonian InstituteFBI Academy (formerly)US Army Rangers(formerly)US Special Forces(formerly)US Army (formerly)Physical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:MaleRelationshipsFamily:John Wilkes Booth†(ancestor)Temperance Brennan(wife)Parker Booth(son)Christine Booth(daughter)Hank Booth Jr.(son)Allies:Ichabod CraneAbbie MillsAugust Corbin†Enemies:Undead Redcoats†AppearancesPortrayed by:David BoreanazFirst appeared:"The Resurrection in the Remains"Last appeared:"Dead Men Tell No Tales"Gallery US Army Ranger (former) FBI Liaison to the Jeffersonian InstituteFBI Academy instructor(briefly)Prison Inmate(briefly)Sergeant Major/Master Sergeant (formerly) Generally, Booth has a cheerful, happy-go-lucky personality. He frequently smiles, makes jokes, and occasionally acts in a silly, almost childish manner. On the job, he tends to adopt a more serious, professional attitude, although his cheerful side occasionally slips through. However, he also has issues with his temperament and, as shown in several episodes, it has gotten him in trouble on occasion. When it comes to solving crimes, Booth has a very different approach from his wife Dr. Brennan and her team, preferring a more human, interpersonal, and intuitive set of methods. While he finds the information Dr. Brennan and her team uncover valuable evidence, he often finds their means overly convoluted and restrictive and adds his intuition and knowledge of people on top of it — something that clashes with Dr. Brennan's hard, objective and analytical approach which is mindful of its own limits. Booth is a patriot and has a strong sense of duty to his country and job. In "Soldier on the Grave", Angela described him as "someone who wants to keep honor and responsibility alive". For much of Season 1, this often led to friction between him and Jack Hodgins, who held anti-government views. According to Cam, Booth relies on his faith in the government to keep his sanity intact having killed nearly fifty people on government orders as an Army sniper. He takes his oath and position as a federal agent seriously and holds himself to an equally high standard; he is especially disgusted whenever a corrupt law enforcement officer is involved and refuses to participate in cover-ups. Booth is also fiercely loyal and protective towards his friends and family to the point where he will not hesitate to physically threaten and intimidate anyone who attempts to harm them. Sweets theorized that his protective instincts – which he labeled "white knight syndrome" – stems from his abusive childhood and his having to frequently protect his younger brother Jared from their alcoholic father. His former boss Sam Cullen called him

characters.name: Mercy Bradford
characters.description: Mercy BradfordBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedPosition:WitnessPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:FemaleHair color:BrownEye color:BrownRelationshipsFamily:William Bradford(father)†Enemies:TheBarghest†AppearancesPortrayed by:Aurora McBethFirst appeared:"Loco Parentis"GalleryMercy Bradfordwas a member of the Massachusetts Bay Colony and aWitness.  She was attacked and killed by theBarghestwhen she inherited her mantle on her 11th birthday. Mercy Bradford's father had described her as "special." He wrote in his journal that she was one of "two olive trees that stand before the Lord." This is the biblical description of the two Witnesses.  According to the Chronicles, she was killed on her 11th birthday because that is when the Witnesses acquire their powerful mantle.

characters.name: William Bradford
characters.description: William BradfordBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedPosition:CaptainPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:MaleHair color:BrownEye color:BrownRelationshipsFamily:Mercy Bradford†(daughter)Enemies:TheBarghest†AppearancesPortrayed by:Brent McGeeFirst appeared:"Loco Parentis"Gallery CaptainWilliam Bradfordwas a character in the Middle Ages who was the father ofWitnessMercy Bradford. He was killed by theBarghest. William had a daughter calledMercy Bradfordwho was aWitness. On her eleventh birthday, she inherited the full responsibility and power of a witness but was subsequently killed by a demonic wolf, theBarghest. After her death, he wrote in his journal about her and the creature that killed her.[1]

characters.name: Temperance Brennan
characters.description: Temperance BrennanBiographical InformationBirth:1976Alias:BonesTempeBrenWanda MoosejawRoxy ScallionStatus:AliveProfession:Forensic AnthropologistNovelistAffiliation:Jeffersonian InstituteFBIPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:FemaleRelationshipsFamily:Max Keenan†(father)Christine Brennan†(mother)Russ Brennan(brother)Seeley Booth(husband)Christine Booth(daughter)Hank Booth II(son)Parker Booth(stepson)Allies:Seeley BoothIchabod CraneAbbie MillsEnemies:William HoweUndead RedcoatsAppearancesPortrayed by:Emily DeschanelFirst appeared:"The Resurrection in the Remains"Last appeared:"Dead Men Tell No Tales"GalleryTemperance "Bones" Brennan(bornJoy Keenan), Ph.D. is a main character in the American Fox television seriesBones. Christine Brennan†(mother)Russ Brennan(brother)Seeley Booth(husband)Christine Booth(daughter)Hank Booth II(son)Parker Booth(stepson) She appeared in theSleepy Hollowepisode "Dead Men Tell No Tales", which was the second half of a two-part crossover between the showBonesand Sleepy Hollow which began with the Bones episode "The Resurrection in the Remains". Throughout the course of the "Bones"series, Brennan is portrayed as a straightforward, brilliant anthropologist, who lacks social skills. Her social ineptitude is especially apparent when it comes to sarcasm, metaphors which she often interprets literally, and pop culture jokes and is often the source of comedy in the show. In earlier seasons, she was characterized as straightforward and unable to detect social cues – she states that Booth once told her that she "stinks at non-verbal communication" – and was well-known within the FBI for being extremely difficult to work with. She began to acknowledge her lack of sensitivity after Booth bluntly told her outright that she was "bad with people" in "A Boy in a Tree". Her lack of "political savvy" and social skills was also a reason why she was passed over for Camille Saroyan as head of forensic division in the Jeffersonian in Season 2. Other characters have described her as "no fun" and "a rigid traditionalist". She had a difficult adolescence, and it is implied, often by Sweets, that her withdrawn social tendencies are a defense mechanism. She also sometimes struggles in identifying and explaining her emotions, and takes comfort in the rationality of her anthropological discipline. Brennan's personality undergoes significant changes throughout the course of the series. Her thinking becomes less rigid in later seasons, something which is observed by Dr. Gordon Wyatt, who notes that she is now able to distinguish the difference between accuracy and truth. She is also able to put aside her rationality to support her friends in sometimes irrational pursuits, and to comfort Booth, even using science or quoting directly from the Bible to rationalize his religious beliefs. Her sensitivity and empathy towards others are also much improved, seen quite strongly when she comforts his grandfather,and when she attends a funeral so that the vic

characters.name: Andy Brooks
characters.description: Andy BrooksBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedProfession:Police officer(formerly)Position:NecromancerAffiliation:Sleepy Hollow Sheriff Department(formerly)Moloch(formerly)Physical DescriptionSpecies:HumanDemon(formerly)Gender:MaleHair color:BlackEye color:BrownRelationshipsAllies:*Abbie Mills†Ichabod CraneAbraham Van Brunt(formerly)Moloch†(formerly)Serilda of Abaddon†Enemies:*Ichabod Crane(formerly)Luke Morales†Moloch†AppearancesPortrayed by:John ChoFirst appeared:"Pilot"Last appeared:"This Is War"GalleryAndy Brookswas a former police officer of theSleepy Hollow Sheriff Department. He sold his soul to Moloch and became one of his henchman. After failing a duty, he was instantly killed byMolochand resurrected later and given a second chance to continue Moloch's evil plans. Although Andy is Moloch's ally, he attempts to assistAbbie, an old friend he claims to genuinely care about. He was imprisoned by Moloch inPurgatoryafter his second death inThe Indispensable Man. Due to Moloch's death, it is possible that Andy has been freed from Purgatory. Andy lived inSleepy Hollowmost of his life, and attended the same college asAbbie.[1]Like Abbie, Andy followed the path to becoming a police officer at theSleepy Hollow Sheriff Department. At some point in Andy's life, he sold his soul toMoloch, becoming his unwilling henchman. Andy gave off the impression that he was a man of the law, concerned for others' protection. Being Moloch's assistant, Andy wasn't necessarily evil, but only prophesied to do evil things. As shown before, when given a second chance to fulfill Moloch's plans, he seemed very reluctant to make a very young target out ofSerilda'svictim, but had no choice.[3]Andy had also been shown to be quite obsessive, specifically over Abbie, and would go by whatever measures to protect her, even if it meant getting destroyed by Moloch. Although he sold his soul, Andy was very unhappy as Moloch's servant, and begged for his own death.[1]He kept in touch with some morals, and helped Abbie in times of need, knowing it would be a step closer to helping her stop Moloch.

Being Moloch's assistant, Andy wasn't necessarily evil, but only prophesied to do evil things. As shown before, when given a second chance to fulfill Moloch's plans, he seemed very reluctant to make a very young target out ofSerilda'svictim, but had no choice.[3]Andy had also been shown to be quite obsessive, specifically over Abbie, and would go by whatever measures to protect her, even if it meant getting destroyed by Moloch. Although he sold his soul, Andy was very unhappy as Moloch's servant, and begged for his own death.[1]He kept in touch with some morals, and helped Abbie in times of need, knowing it would be a step closer to helping her stop Moloch.

characters.name: Carlton
characters.description: CarltonBiographical InformationStatus:AliveProfession:HessianHunterPosition:Shadow WarriorAffiliation:Shadow WarriorsPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:MaleHair color:BlondeEye color:BlueRelationshipsAllies:Shadow Warriors,Moloch,Abraham Van BruntAppearancesPortrayed by:Jayson Warner SmithFirst appeared:""Necromancer"(only appearance)"GalleryCarltonis a member of theShadow Warriors. While hunting with another man,Fuller, Carlton spottedthe  Headless Horseman's mount in the woods and appeared to recognize the other-wordly horse for what it was. Using his cell phone, he immediately and urgently contacted  someone and notified him that he had found the horse in the woods, but the horse's rider was missing. Fuller was confused by Carlton's concern, and the fact that he seemed not to be speaking English, unaware that his supposed friend was actually aShadow Warrior. When he then asked if Carlton had been speaking German. Without replying, Carlton casually, and without remorse, shot him to death.[1]

characters.name: Caroline
characters.description: CarolineBiographical InformationStatus:DeceasedProfession:Re-enactorSeamstressPhysical DescriptionSpecies:HumanGender:FemaleHair color:AuburnEye color:BlueRelationshipsAllies:Ichabod CraneEnemies:Mary WellsAppearancesPortrayed by:Laura SpencerFirst appeared:"Bad Blood"Last appeared:"The Weeping Lady"Gallery Carolinewas a Colonial re-enactor with a passion for history. She met Crane at a re-enectment when he mistook the costumed woman for his wife. She found his dedication to 're-enacting' inspired and supplied Crane with eighteenth century-style clothing, and spent time with him, developing a crush on the man out of time. When she finally tried to act on her feelings for him, she startled the confession that he was married from him, embarrassing her. He would go out of his way to make amends, and tell her he appreciated her friendship. She was killed by theWeeping Ladyshortly thereafter. Caroline was first seen walking across a field, her red hair showing beneath a mob cap. Crane saw her in 18th style clothing and, thinking that she was his wife shouted her name, to which she did not respond.  Caroline just kept walking until she passed through a crowd of men in uniforms from the Revolutionary War, much to Crane's astonishment and confusion.
